data.title: Banco Santander Q1: Good, But Still Not Enough Again
data.description:
Investment Thesis

I recommend holding Santander Brasil (NYSE:BSBR) shares after the release of its Q1 2025 results. This article is a continuation of my initial coverage article published on May 6, 2024, with a sell recommendation at the time.

Next, I will perform several analyses on each segment of the results reported by Santander Brasil, and I will link this with my investment thesis and the valuation compared to peers.

Review of Santander Brasil Results In 1Q25

Banco Santander Brasil released its results for the first quarter of 2025 on April 30, and after successive disappointments, the bank finally delivered results within market expectations, both for net revenue and net profit.

I will analyze each segment of the results now. Additionally, it is interesting that the bank operates in Brazil and discloses its results in Brazilian currency, so I will convert them to dollars using the exchange rate of 1 USD = 5.72 BRL, since this was the exchange rate on the last day of the quarter.

Expanded Loan Portfolio

The loan portfolio varied +4% y/y and -0.6% q/q to BRL 546 billion ($95 billion), however, if the exchange rate variation is disregarded, the portfolio would have expanded 0.6% q/q. The positive highlights were Consumer Finance growing 15.7% y/y and SMEs growing 13.2% y/y, the negative highlights were Corporate falling 3.8%.

In my view, the annual growth of the loan portfolio was quite modest, and the quarterly decline shows Santander Brasil's caution in expanding its credit portfolio, which reflects its more selective strategy. As for the outlook, I see that the selective strategy will continue until interest rates return to lower levels, so I do not see strong growth ahead.

Non-Performing Loans

Short-term Non-Performing Loans (NPLs) (15–90 days) rose 40 bps q/q and 30 bps y/y, driven by deterioration in individual segment (+80 bps q/q and 50 bps y/y) justified by more restrictive negotiations and seasonality. Non-Performing Loans (NPLs) over 90 days increased by +10 bps q/q and +10 bps y/y, with a focus on SMEs (+40 bps q/q and +50 bps y/y).

This slight deterioration was expected due to the still challenging credit scenario, especially in retail. I do not believe in an abrupt improvement in this indicator, but rather a gradual one, as the environment for interest rate cuts becomes more evident. The risk remains for low-income individuals.

Net Interest Income

The big interest here is NII (Net Interest Income) from clients, which varied +0.3% q/q and +9.5% y/y to BRL 15.8 billion ($2.76 billion). This was due to higher spreads and higher average volume (+1.6% q/q).

The margin with customers remains resilient and dynamic. Since there is no prospect of a reduction in interest rates in the short term, any improvement here will be modest. Furthermore, this improvement will be constrained if NPLs deteriorate.

Allowance For Loan Losses And Cost Of Risk

Managerial allowance for loan losses increased 7.7% q/q and 5.7% y/y to BRL 6.39 bi ($1.21 bi) impacted by the adoption of the expected loss model. Cost of credit varied +20 bps q/q and -10 bps y/y to 3.7% as we can see below.

The increase in provisions is a point of attention in the result, as it reflects the worsening of credit with the new regulatory measure adopted. In my view, the cost of risk will remain high in the short term.

Expenses

Administrative expenses varied -2.9% q/q and +4.4% y/y to BRL 6.57 billion ($1.15 billion). The efficiency ratio varied -0.8% q/q and -2.5% y/y and closed at 37.2%, being a positive highlight of the result,

Once again, the bank demonstrated excellent cost control, as reflected in the improvement in its efficiency ratio. In my view, there is room for further efficiency improvements by reducing the number of physical branches. It is important for the bank to control its expenses well while Brazil is in a complex macroeconomic environment.

Managerial Net Profit

Net income increased 0.2% q/q and 27.8% y/y to BRL 3.86 billion, ROE also increased from 13.8% in 1Q24 to 17.4% in 1Q25.

The quarterly variation is interesting, as it shows stability even amid rising defaults and inflationary pressures. In my view, future profits should remain resilient, but I do not expect a major acceleration, which corroborates a less than optimistic view of the thesis.

Overall, Santander Brasil reported interesting but not spectacular results. The bank managed to maintain high profitability, despite some pressure on defaults and a less than encouraging macroeconomic outlook. Now, let's analyze the bank's valuation.

Valuation

It is important to note that there has been a substantial improvement in Santander's valuation. The bank is currently trading at a P/E of 7x versus Itaú's P/E of 8.45x. In addition, the profit margin is very similar.

However, Itaú (NYSE:ITUB) still has higher quality results in my view, as I will discuss shortly in its earnings coverage. In addition, when we add the average P/E of its peers, we arrive at 6.5x, so Santander's P/E still presents a 7% premium to its peers, which corroborates the recommendation to hold the shares. I am more confident in the recommendation when I see that SA's powerful Quant tools also recommend that the investor hold their shares.

Potential Threats To The Thesis

There are several risks to the thesis. This is what has happened since my last article, and with the general improvement in the momentum of Brazilian assets, Santander Brasil shares have also risen.

I also see an intrinsic risk to the thesis. As we saw in the valuation chapter, the company has good margins and returns, but its shares have fallen by 14.80% in the last year. This may encourage investors to buy, believing that the shares can close the gap against their competitors.

The Bottom Line

Santander Brasil delivered satisfactory results, especially when we analyze the complex Brazilian macroeconomic scenario. Therefore, the strategy of selective growth in the loan portfolio and conservatism are valid.

However, these results are still not enough to change my recommendation, even with the improvement in momentum in the shares. In the current scenario, I still prefer more robust investment cases, even if I pay a little more for it.

Based on this analysis, I reiterate my recommendation to hold Santander Brasil shares. It is undeniable that the risk/return ratio has been improving, but it is still not enough to raise the recommendation.

data.title: IEUR: Europe Hitting New Highs, Still Cheap
data.description:
Larry Summers’ famous quip, “Europe's a museum, Japan's a nursing home and China's a jail,” points a bearish finger across the pond. The major European bourses have, however, collectively outperformed the S&P 500 since the bear-market bottom of October 2023...if you back out NVIDIA (NVDA).

And so far this year, it has been a European rout when comparing returns to the underperforming US stock market. I have been outspoken in recent years, asserting that ex-US stocks not only have offered high dividends, but were also attractively priced on an absolute and relative basis.

Today, let’s zoom in on the iShares Core MSCI Europe ETF (NYSEARCA:IEUR). The fund is near all-time highs, and I have a buy rating on it.

Europe Beating the US ex-NVDA Since October 2022

According to the issuer, IEUR seeks to track the investment results of an index composed of European equities, offering investors exposure to a broad range of European companies. It’s a low-cost comprehensive Euro Area ETF, holding large-, mid-, and small-cap stocks (the "Investable Market") within developed market countries in Europe.

IEUR is a medium-sized ETF with $6.0 billion in assets under management as of May 7, 2025. Its annual expense ratio is low at just nine basis points, while the forward dividend yield is almost a full percentage point above that of the S&P 500 at 2.27%. Share-price momentum has been stellar this year, with the fund returning a whopping 17.4% through this past Wednesday—more than 20 percentage points of alpha versus the S&P 500 Trust ETF (SPY), dividends included.

IEUR has a lukewarm ETF Risk Grade by Seeking Alpha’s quantitative scoring system, as it can be volatile, particularly when the EUR:USD currency pair moves lower. Liquidity is healthy, though, as evidenced by its low median 30-day bid/ask spread of just three basis points, per iShares. Average daily volume is high at 1.06 million.

Looking closer at the portfolio, the 3-star ETF by Morningstar plots along the top row of the style box, indicating its bent to the large-cap space. There’s actually a fractional overweight to growth over other value, which may surprise prospective investors. Just 21% of IEUR is invested in SMID caps, so that limits cyclical risks.

With a price-to-earnings ratio of just 14x, the nominal valuation is compelling. Of course, the long-term EPS growth rate is not all that high at 8.4%, but that still yields a PEG ratio under the US market’s.

IEUR: Portfolio & Factor Profiles

European bank stocks have taken off in 2025, but the industry’s rally dates back to 2022. Financials is indeed the biggest sector with IEUR, while Information Technology, among the worst-performing YTD sectors, is just 8% of the product. Sector mix plays a key role in performance, and most sectors are cheaper in Europe than their US counterparts.

Moreover, if you seek diversification, IEUR is not a bad place to look. The top 10 holdings account for just 17.5% of the ETF.

IEUR: Holdings & Dividend Information

Seasonally, we have to be a bit on guard. June has historically been the worst month, while returns are strong in July when analyzing the past 10 years of performance data.

IEUR: Weak June Seasonality Is A Risk

The Technical Take

With a low valuation and intense relative strength in 2025, IEUR’s chart is mixed, but with high momentum. Notice in the graph below that shares are now at resistance, at least according to a multi-year uptrend line. Still, with the fund basically at all-time highs, I err to the bullish side here. A cautionary signal is seen, however, with the RSI momentum oscillator at the top of the chart—it has printed a negative divergence to price in the last handful of sessions. I’d like to see the RSI hit new highs along with price to negate that yellow flag.

But take a look at the volume by price profiles on the left side of the graph—there should be ample cushion if we see a pullback, given the number of shares traded down to the low $40s. I also see support at $53—the 2025 lows. Finally, the long-term 200-day moving average is trending up and is below the current price; those are bullish features that the S&P 500 cannot boast.

IEUR: Long-Term Uptrend, New Highs, Rising 200dma, $53 Support

The Bottom Line

I have a buy rating on IEUR. I see the European ETF as still attractive on valuation, while its momentum is very high with intense alpha so far in 2025.

data.title: Tracking Cathie Wood's ARK Invest 13F Portfolio - Q1 2025 Update
data.description:
This article is part of a series that provides an ongoing analysis of the changes made to ARK Invest’ 13F portfolio on a quarterly basis. It is based on their regulatory 13F Form filed on 05/07/2025.

ARK Invest was founded by Cathie Wood in 2014. They manage several actively managed ETFs, index ETFs, and certain other international products. Assets Under Management has come down from over $50B at the peak to ~$10B now. They invest in what they term “disruptive innovation”. The actively managed ETFs are ARK Innovation ETF (BATS:ARKK), ARK Autonomous Tech & Robotics ETF (BATS:ARKQ), ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (BATS:ARKW), ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (BATS:ARKG), ARK Fintech Innovation ETF (BATS:ARKF), ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (BATS:ARKX), and ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF (BATS:ARKB).

This quarter, ARK Invest’ 13F portfolio value decreased from ~$12B to ~$10B. The number of holdings increased from 183 to 196. There are 41 securities that are significantly large, and they are the focus of this article. The top three holdings are at ~20% while the top five are at ~30% of the 13F assets: Tesla, Roku, Palantir, Roblox, and Coinbase Global. Please visit our previous update for the fund’s moves during Q4 2024.

Note 1: Unlike other investment management businesses, ARK is pioneering an open-source model of investment research through a couple of initiatives: a) Valuation models on businesses are being made available to the public through GitHub, and b) daily trades are available to anyone who signs up for it. The open source model along with the outlandish forecasts has attracted criticism as well: in April 2022, RIA lawyers urged SEC enforcement action.

Note 2: The 13F data on which this article is based is as of 03/31/2025. Updated daily holdings data for each of their ETFs are publicly available.

Note 3: Although as a percentage of the overall portfolio the positions are very small, it is significant that they have sizable ownership stakes in the following businesses: 908 Devices (MASS), Absci Corp (ABSI), Adaptive Biotech (ADPT), Blade Air Mobility (BLDE), Butterfly Network (BFLY), Cerus Corp. (CERS), Nextdoor Holdings (KIND), Personalis (PSNL), Quantum-Si (QSI), and Repare Therapeutics (RPTX).

Stake Increases:

Coinbase Global (COIN): COIN had an IPO in April 2021. Shares started trading at ~$290 and currently go for ~$200. The position was built during Q2 & Q3 2021 at prices between ~$225 and ~$342. Q4 2021 saw a ~22% trimming at prices between ~$231 and ~$343 while the next two quarters saw a two-thirds stake increase at prices between ~$49 and ~$252. The two quarters through Q1 2023 saw another ~55% stake increase at prices between ~$32 and ~$82. The stake was decreased by 13% during Q3 2023 at prices between ~$71 and ~$110. That was followed by a ~19% reduction during the next quarter at prices between ~$71 and ~$186. Q1 2024 saw a further ~50% selling at prices between ~$117 and ~$280. That was followed by a ~20% reduction during the next quarter at prices between ~$199 and ~$263. The last two quarters also saw a ~15% trimming. The stake is still a top five position at 5.29% of the portfolio. There was a marginal increase this quarter.

Tempus AI (TEM): TEM had an IPO in June 2024. Shares started trading at ~$40 and currently goes for $65.22. The position was built during Q3 2024 at prices between $30.88 and $71.47. There was a ~130% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between $33.03 and $77.62. That was followed by a ~35% increase this quarter at prices between ~$32 and ~$89. The stake is now at 3.62% of the portfolio.

CRISPR Therapeutics AG (CRSP): The bulk of the current 3.46% of the portfolio position in CRSP was built in 2020 at prices between ~$38 and ~$169. The stake has wavered. Q1 2021 saw a ~20% selling while in Q4 2021 there was a similar increase. There was a ~20% stake increase during Q4 2023 at prices between $38.62 and $72.18. The last quarter saw a similar increase at prices between $38.65 and $55.15. That was followed by a ~13% increase this quarter at prices between ~$34 and ~$53. The stock is now at $36.14.

Note: They have a ~10.5% ownership stake in CRISPR Therapeutics.

Amazon.com (AMZN): The ~2% AMZN position saw a whopping ~160% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$181 and ~$233. There was a ~18% stake increase this quarter at prices between ~$190 and ~$242. The stock is now at ~$192.

Beam Therapeutics (BEAM): BEAM is a 1.66% of the portfolio position built over the seven quarters through Q2 2022 at prices between ~$22 and ~$130. There was a ~7% increase during Q4 2023 at prices between $17.69 and $30.76. The stake was decreased by 13% in the next quarter at prices between $23.46 and $45.07. The stock is now at $17.25. Q2 & Q3 2024 saw another ~20% selling while this quarter there was a similar stake increase at prices between ~$18 and ~$34.

Note: they have a ~9.5% ownership stake in Beam Therapeutics.

Advanced Micro Devices (AMD): The small 1.46% AMD stake was built during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$129 and ~$184. The last quarter saw a ~55% stake increase at prices between ~$119 and ~$173. That was followed by a ~18% stake increase this quarter at prices between ~$97 and ~$130. The stock currently trades at ~$102.

Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA): NTLA was a minutely small position in ARK’s first 13F filing in 2016. The 2017-2020 time period saw the position built to a ~11.2M share position at prices between ~$12.50 and ~$62. Since then, the stake has wavered. The first three quarters of 2021 saw a ~40% selling at prices between ~$52 and ~$177 while the next four quarters saw a similar increase at prices between ~$38 and ~$138. Q4 2023 saw a ~15% increase at prices between $23.16 and $32.34. The last few quarters saw minor increases. The stock currently trades at $8.28, and the stake is at 0.93% of the portfolio.

Note: they have a ~14.5% ownership stake in Intellia Therapeutics.

Illumina Inc. (ILMN): ILMN is a small 0.71% of the portfolio stake established during the last quarter at prices between ~$129 and ~$155. There was a ~30% stake increase this quarter at prices between ~$79 and ~$144. The stock currently trades at $79.58.

10X Genomics (TXG), Deere & Co (DE), GitLab Inc. (GTLB), Iridium Communications (IRDM), Natera Inc. (NTRA), Pacific Biosciences (PACB), PagerDuty (PD), Recursion Pharma (RXRX), Twist Bioscience (TWST), Teradyne Inc. (TER), Trade Desk (TTD), and Trimble Inc. (TRMB): These positions (less than ~2.5% of the portfolio each) were increased during the quarter.

Note: They have significant ownership stakes in the following businesses: 10X Genomics, Pacific Biosciences, and Twist Bioscience.

Stake Decreases:

Tesla Inc. (TSLA): TSLA is currently the largest 13F position at 8.45% of the portfolio. It was already a small position in their first 13F filing in 2016. Recent activity follows. Q1 2021 saw a ~40% stake increase at prices between ~$199 and ~$293. The six quarters through Q3 2022 had seen the position reduced by ~80% at prices between ~$217 and ~$407. The two quarters through Q1 2023 saw a ~30% stake increase while in the next two quarters there was a similar reduction. Q4 2023 saw a ~7% trimming at prices between ~$197 and ~$264 while in the next quarter there was a ~36% increase at prices between ~$163 and ~$248. Q3 2024 saw a ~14% selling at prices between ~$192 and ~$263. That was followed by a ~28% reduction during the last quarter at prices between ~$214 and ~$480. The stock currently trades at ~$285. This quarter saw marginal trimming.

Roku (ROKU): ROKU is a large (top three) ~6% of the portfolio position built in the 2019-20 period at prices between ~$33 and ~$357. The four quarters through Q3 2022 saw another ~150% stake increase at prices between ~$56 and ~$345. The stake was decreased by 13% during Q3 2023 at prices between $61.60 and $97.49. Q4 2023 saw a ~8% selling at prices between ~$56 and ~$107. The position was increased by 32% in the next quarter at prices between $62.67 and $98.57. Q3 2024 saw a ~12% trimming at prices between $51.67 and $77.45. That was followed by a ~20% reduction this quarter at prices between ~$67 and ~$99. The stock is now at $61.36.

Note: they have a ~7% ownership stake in Roku.

Palantir Technologies (PLTR): The large (top three) 5.31% stake in PLTR was established during Q2 2023 at prices between ~$7.40 and ~$16.60. There was a ~38% stake increase during Q3 2023 at prices between $13.96 and $19.99. That was followed by a ~16% increase in the next quarter at prices between $14.69 and $21.34. Q2 2024 also saw a similar increase at prices between $15.98 and $26.46. The next quarter saw a ~15% trimming at prices between $24.09 and $37.95. That was followed by a ~30% reduction during the last quarter at prices between $36.46 and $82.38. This quarter also saw a ~20% selling at prices between ~$65 and ~$125. The stock currently trades at ~$119.

Roblox Corp (RBLX): The 5.29% (top five) RBLX position was built during the four quarters through Q3 2022 at prices between ~$23 and ~$135. There was a ~10% stake increase in H1 2023. That was followed by a ~28% increase during Q3 2023 at prices between $25.31 and $45.54. There was a ~14% selling during the last quarter at prices between $39.86 and $61.45. This quarter also saw a ~11% trimming at prices between ~$54 and ~$76. The stock currently trades at $70.05.

Robinhood Markets (HOOD): HOOD had an IPO in August 2021. Shares started trading at ~$55 and currently go for $54.07. The 4.27% of the portfolio position was built through consistent buying over the four quarters through Q2 2022 at prices between ~$7 and ~$55. The stake was decreased by 25% during Q1 2024 at prices between $10.56 and $20.13. The next quarter saw another ~27% selling at prices between $16.23 and $23.70. That was followed by a similar reduction during the last quarter at prices between $22.33 and $43.20. This quarter also saw a ~20% selling at prices between ~$36 and ~$65.

Shopify (SHOP): The bulk of the current 4.25% position in SHOP was built during the three quarters through Q2 2021 at prices between ~$92 and ~$147. The stake has since wavered. H2 2021 saw a ~40% reduction at prices between ~$135 and ~$169 while the next three quarters saw a stake doubling at prices between ~$27 and ~$136. There was a ~37% reduction during Q2 2023 at prices between ~$45 and ~$66. The stake was decreased by another ~22% in the next quarter at prices between $51.51 and $70.37. Q4 2023 also saw a ~14% selling at prices between $46.40 and $79.11. The stake was decreased by ~24% in the next quarter at prices between $71.82 and $90.72. The last four quarters saw only minor adjustments. The stock is now at ~$94.

Meta Platforms (META): META position saw a ~40% stake increase during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$345 and ~$505. That was followed by a ~17% increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$554 and ~$632. This quarter saw a ~10% reduction at prices between ~$576 and ~$737. The stock currently trades at ~$598. The stake is now at ~2.4% of the portfolio.

Archer Aviation (ACHR): The 1.94% ACHR stake saw a ~27% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between $2.94 and $11.33. The stock currently trades at $8.76. There was a ~9% trimming this quarter. They have a ~8% ownership stake in the business.

DraftKings Inc. (DKNG): DKNG came to market through a De-SPAC transaction in Q1 2021. The 1.61% of the portfolio stake was built through consistent buying every quarter at prices up to ~$70. Q2 2023 saw a ~25% selling at prices between ~$18 and ~$27. That was followed by a ~19% reduction during Q3 2023 at prices between $25.23 and $32.38. The stake was decreased by 16% during Q4 2023 at prices between $26.22 and $39. That was followed by a ~30% reduction in the next quarter at prices between $32.21 and $48.68. Q2 2024 also saw another similar reduction at prices between $35.13 and $47.20. That was followed by a ~13% trimming this quarter at prices between ~$33 and ~$54. The stock is now at $35.35.

UiPath Inc. (PATH): PATH had an IPO in April 2021. Shares started trading at ~$72 and currently go for $12.44. The position was built in the Q2 to Q3 2021 period at prices between ~$52 and ~$80. The four quarters through Q3 2022 saw the position almost double at prices between ~$12.50 and ~$56. H1 2023 saw a ~10% increase, while Q4 2023 saw a ~8% reduction at prices between $14.94 and $26.26. The stake was decreased by 27% in the next quarter at prices between $21.60 and $26.88. That was followed by another ~30% selling during Q3 2024 at prices between $10.75 and $13.24. This quarter saw a ~60% reduction at prices between ~$10 and ~$15. The stake is now at 0.86% of the portfolio.

Adaptive Biotechnologies (ADPT), ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF (ARKB), Block Inc. (XYZ), Guardant Health (GH), Kratos Defense & Security (KTOS), MercadoLibre (MELI), Pinterest Inc. (PINS), Rocket Lab (RKLB), SoFi Technologies (SOFI), Toast Inc. (TOST), and Veracyte Inc. (VCYT): These stakes (less than ~2.5% of the portfolio each) were reduced during the quarter.

Note: they have significant ownership stakes in the following businesses – Kratos Defense & Security, Rocket Lab, and Veracyte.

The spreadsheet below highlights changes to ARK Invest’ 13F holdings in Q1 2025:

Source: John Vincent. Data constructed from ARK Invest’ 13F filings for Q4 2024 and Q1 2025.

data.title: American Century Disciplined Core Value Fund: Very Attractive In A Turbulent Market
data.description:
Our ETFs and mutual funds Ratings are based on our Stock Ratings. After all, a fund is only as good as the stocks it holds, so it makes sense that to properly evaluate a fund, one must evaluate each of the fund’s holdings. It also makes sense that if Very Attractive Rated Stocks as a group can outperform, then so should the Very Attractive-rated mutual funds.

After rigorous analysis of our database of 7,600+ ETFs and mutual funds, we found a mutual fund that earns our Very Attractive Predictive Fund Rating. It earns this rating because it picks stocks with quality Risk/Reward and charges below-average fees. American Century Disciplined Core Value Fund (MUTF:BIGRX) is this week’s Long Idea.

Forward-Looking Research Reveals a Very Attractive Fund

While Morningstar gives BIGRX a 2-Star (backward-looking) rating, our analysis of its holdings reveals the fund allocates more to good stocks, i.e. profitable companies with undervalued stock prices, than its benchmark, iShares Core S&P U.S. Value ETF (IUSV). See Figure 1.

We leverage our Robo-Analyst technology to assess a mutual fund’s portfolio quality by analyzing the fund’s individual stock holdings.

Through this rigorous analysis, we find that BIGRX allocates 36% of its assets to Attractive-or-better rated stocks, compared to just 15% for IUSV. On the flip side, BIGRX allocates just 19% of its assets to Unattractive-or-worse rated stocks, compared to 33% for IUSV.

Figure 1: BIGRX Holdings Vs. IUSV Holdings

Sources: New Constructs, LLC, company, ETF, and mutual fund filings

Per Figure 2, our holdings analysis also reveals BIGRX’s portfolio is of much higher quality than the S&P 500 as represented by State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY). SPY earns our Attractive rating, but only 10% of SPY’s portfolio is allocated to stocks rated Attractive-or-better and 31% is allocated to stocks rated Unattractive-or-worse.

Figure 2: BIGRX Holdings Vs. SPY Holdings

Sources: New Constructs, LLC, company, ETF, and mutual fund filings

The Fund’s Strategy Finds Companies with Quality Risk/Reward

BIGRX’s investment strategy, led by American Century Investments, aims to create a portfolio of primarily large cap U.S. stocks with a focus on long-term capital growth, that also provides a dividend yield greater than that of the S&P 500. Managers use quantitative and qualitative management techniques in a multistep process, based on objective measures which include:

  • valuation (ratios which look at a firm’s value relative to cash flow),
  • quality (profitability and earnings sustainability),
  • growth (rate of growth of a company’s earnings), and
  • and sentiment (historical stock returns, share volume, and options data).

Based on the output of the quantitative model, managers then consider factors such as economic events, corporate announcements, risk management, transaction costs, and liquidity to select stocks.

BIGRX’s managers’ belief in the importance of sustainable earnings and valuation relative to cash flows would appear to resemble two key drivers of our Stock Ratings, quality of earnings and valuation. Without detailed information regarding which specific metrics American Century Investments uses for its stock-picking criteria, we can only judge the effectiveness of their stated strategy by analyzing the holdings of the mutual fund.

In doing so, it’s clear the fund allocates to companies with quality overall Risk/Reward.

Given the fund’s superior asset allocation (compared to its benchmark and the SPY), we think the managers successfully implement their philosophy of finding stocks that “provide a balance between risk and return”, as we’ll detail in our holdings analysis below.

Quality Stocks Drive Very Attractive Risk/Reward Rating

Figure 3 shows our detailed rating for BIGRX, which includes each of the criteria we use to rate all mutual funds under coverage. These criteria are the same for our Stock Rating Methodology because the performance of a mutual fund’s holdings drives the performance of the mutual fund after fees. Figure 3 also compares BIGRX’s rating with those of IUSV, SPY and IWM.

Figure 3: American Century Disciplined Core Value Fund Breakdown

Sources: New Constructs, LLC and company filings

BIGRX’s holdings are superior or equal to IUSV in all five, and superior or equal to SPY in four out of five of the criteria that make up our Portfolio Management rating. Specifically:

  • BIGRX’s holdings generate positive economic earnings, same as IUSV and SPY.
  • BIGRX’s ROIC is 18% and higher than the 16% earned by IUSV’s holdings, but lower than the 33% earned by SPY’s holdings.
  • BIGRX’s free cash flow (FCF) yield of 4% is higher than IUSV and SPY’s at 2%.
  • The price-to-economic book value (PEBV) ratio for BIGRX’s holdings is 1.9, which is much lower than IUSV’s at 2.6 and SPY’s at 3.2.
  • Our discounted cash flow analysis reveals an average market-implied growth appreciation period (GAP) of just 37 years for BIGRX’s holdings, compared to 50 years for IUSV and 66 years for SPY.

Market expectations for stocks held by BIGRX imply profits will grow much less than the stock’s held by IUSV (measured by PEBV ratio and the market-implied GAP).

In other words, BIGRX’s profitability is higher (measured by ROIC), yet the stocks held by BIGRX are significantly cheaper (as measured by PEBV and the market-implied GAP).

A Closer Look at Holdings Reveals More Positives

Of the 199 BIGRX’s holdings in our coverage universe, 6 are also open Long Ideas of ours. These 6 stocks make up nearly 8% of BIGRX’s portfolio.

Also, of the 199 holdings under coverage:

  • 98% have a positive ROIC,
  • 85% have a positive 2-year average free cash flow yield,
  • 60% have a positive PEBV ratio of 1.9 or less, and
  • 62% have a GAP of 37 years or less.

The Importance of Holdings-Based Fund Analysis

Smart fund (or ETF) investing means analyzing each of the holdings of a fund. Simply buying an ETF or mutual fund based on past performance does not necessarily lead to outperformance. Only thorough holdings-based research can help determine if a fund’s methodology leads managers to pick high-quality or low-quality stocks.

Most investors don’t realize they can access superior fundamental research that enables them to overcome inaccuracies, omissions, and biases in legacy fundamental research and data. Our Robo-Analyst technology analyzes the holdings of all 1,084 ETFs and mutual funds in the All Cap Value style and ~7,600+ ETFs and mutual funds under coverage to avoid “the danger within”.

Build A Better Fund: Use our DIY ETF Tool

As we show in The Paradigm Shift to DIY ETFs, new technologies enable investors to create their own fund without any fees while also enabling better, more sophisticated weighting methodologies. For example, if we reallocate the fund’s capital to the companies with the best ROICs, our customized fund allocates:

  • 45% of assets to Attractive-or-better rated stocks (compared to 36% for BIGRX)
  • 11% of assets to Unattractive-or-worse rated stocks (compared to 19% for BIGRX)
